title: Fordia rheophytica
taxonomy: Fordia rheophytica is a plant species within the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Fordia, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia. Fordia rheophytica is a rhizomatous plant with a unique habit of growing in flooded areas. The species is endemic to Borneo and is often found in the wild growing along riverbanks. It is classified as a vulnerable species due to habitat loss and degradation.

intro: Fordia rheophytica, also known as the mountain vine, is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Fordia rheophytica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Traditional herbal preparations made with Fordia rheophytica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis.
